title: Fix PWA 'Add to Home Screen' install prompt not working

requirements.text: Audit and fix the PWA 'Add to Home Screen' install prompt flow. Ensure the `beforeinstallprompt` event is captured reliably before React renders (attach the listener in `index.html` or early in `main.tsx`), stored, and passed to the `useInstallPrompt` hook. Verify the manifest.json meets all Chrome installability criteria (start_url, display: standalone, icons with at least one 192Ă—192 and one 512Ă—512 PNG). Confirm the service worker (sw.js) is registered successfully and does not throw errors on activation. Fix any race conditions between service worker registration and the `beforeinstallprompt` event that may prevent the banner from appearing.
acceptanceCriteria: On Chrome for Android, the install banner appears after the page loads and the beforeinstallprompt event fires.
Tapping the install banner triggers the native browser install prompt.
After accepting or dismissing the install prompt, the banner is hidden and does not reappear.
The service worker registers without console errors and caches the root URL on install.
The manifest.json includes valid 192Ă—192 and 512Ă—512 icon entries, a start_url, and display: standalone.
Chrome DevTools Application panel shows no installability errors or warnings.
On iOS Safari, a banner with Share > Add to Home Screen instructions is shown instead of the native prompt.

requirements.text: Ensure the `beforeinstallprompt` event listener is registered as early as possible â€” ideally in a `<script>` tag inside `index.html` before the React bundle loads â€” so the event is not missed due to React hydration timing. Store the deferred prompt on `window` (e.g., `window.__installPrompt`) and have `useInstallPrompt` read from it on mount.
acceptanceCriteria: The beforeinstallprompt event is captured even on fast-loading devices before React finishes mounting.
The useInstallPrompt hook correctly reads the deferred prompt from window.__installPrompt on mount.
The install banner renders on first load when the browser fires beforeinstallprompt.

constraints: Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths (useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts, main.tsx, src/components/ui).
Do not alter the backend Motoko actor.
Preserve all existing PWA manifest fields (app name 'MediFind', green theme color, standalone display mode).
nonGoals: Adding new diseases or medicines to the backend.
Changing the visual theme or layout of the app.
Implementing push notifications or background sync in the service worker.
Supporting any browser other than Chrome/Edge (Android) and Safari (iOS) for installation.
